rxn_class_summary: Ni-catalyzed electrochemical reductive cross-coupling of trifluoromethyl alkenes with alkyl halides or alkyl-substituted redox-active esters to give gem-difluoroalkenes via allylic defluorinative alkylation
electrochemical_role: Cathodic reduction of NiBr2(diglyme) to Ni(0) active catalyst; cathode also reduces alkyl-Ni(II) species (or benzylic radical) to enable β-fluoride elimination; Zn serves as sacrificial anode

key_intermediates.name: alkyl radical (R•) from SET to alkyl halide or RAE
α-trifluoromethyl benzylic radical (after radical addition to CF3-alkene)
alkyl-Ni(II)X species (benzylic radical captured by Ni(I))
benzylic anion (from cathodic reduction, Pathway II)

electron_flow: Cathode reduces Ni(II) precatalyst to Ni(0), which undergoes SET to alkyl halide/RAE generating R• and Ni(I)X; R• adds to the CF3-alkene to form an α-CF3 benzylic radical that is either captured by Ni(I) to form alkyl-Ni(II) (Pathway I) or further cathodically reduced to a benzylic anion (Pathway II); β-fluoride elimination then delivers the gem-difluoroalkene, with Zn anode supplying the balancing oxidation.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction strongly inhibited (yield drops from 77% to <2% with 0.5–3.0 equiv TEMPO); R-TEMPO adduct (0.076 mmol) isolated, confirming alkyl radical intermediate

probe_reagents.name: (Bromomethyl)cyclopropane (2u)
probe_reagents.observation: Only ring-opened product 4bu (25%) obtained, consistent with cyclopropylcarbinyl radical ring-opening

probe_reagents.name: 6-iodohex-1-ene (alkenyl iodide 2v)
probe_reagents.observation: Cyclized product 4bva and acyclic 4bvb obtained in 5:1 ratio (61% yield), supporting a 5-exo-trig radical cyclization

probe_reagents.name: Acrylamide-containing CF3-alkene 1l (competition)
probe_reagents.observation: Mono- and dialkylation products formed at the CF3-alkene site; acrylamide untouched, showing higher reactivity of trifluoromethyl alkene toward the alkyl radical

notes: Authors propose two divergent pathways after radical addition: (Pathway I) capture of α-CF3 benzylic radical by Ni(I) to form an alkylnickel that undergoes β-F elimination, or (Pathway II) cathodic reduction of the benzylic radical to a carbanion followed by β-F elimination. A Ni(I)/Ni(III) catalytic manifold cannot be excluded. Chemoselectivity observed: alkyl-RAE > alkyl bromide in competition (substrate 4am), but aliphatic (non-aryl) CF3-alkenes are unreactive under standard conditions.
